title: Set --force-rm for podman build to true by default
body:
Since we use buildah containers for the build process, the
user will not know if we have any buildah containers lingering
due to a failed build. Setting this to true by default till
we figure out a better way to solve this.

Depends on https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1163
Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1586

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: This should be ready to go once tests are green.
@mheon @baude something wrong with the "full_vm_testing"? I see something about failure to connect to irc?
@cevich It looks like the tests succeeded, but the postrun script to update IRC failed? Can we set that to not fail the tests if it happens?
@mheon oh, good idea.  I could also turn it off entirely if you prefer, I'm not married to it.

bot, retest this please
@cevich Is there a way to get full_vm_testing to test again?

@rhatdan yes, just follow the details link, then near the top next to `X Failure` is a 're-run' link.  Smash that and it'll do the deed (I already pressed it for you).  If it fails in that IRC thing again, rebase the PR (change on master makes IRC thing optional).
